welcome.title: AutoVersion keeps a history of every save, so nothing ever gets lost.
welcome.body: AutoVersion runs quietly in your menu bar and takes a snapshot every time you save a file in the folders you protect. You can browse old versions, see exactly what changed, and roll any file back with one click.

pickFolder.title: Pick a folder to protect
pickFolder.body: AutoVersion will watch this folder for changes. Common picks are your Documents folder, a project directory, or wherever you keep work-in-progress files.

extensions.title: Which kinds of files should AutoVersion track?
extensions.body: Pick a preset or define your own list. AutoVersion ignores everything else in the folder.

preset.wordHint: Best for writers using Microsoft Word.

confirm.title: All set
confirm.startAtLogin: Start AutoVersion automatically when I log in

folders.emptyFolder: Pick a folder to see its files.
folders.noFiles: No saves yet — edit a file in this folder to create the first snapshot.
folders.noSnapshots: No snapshots yet for this file.
folders.pickFile: Pick a file to see its history.
folders.noDifferences: No differences between these two versions.
folders.restoreConfirm: Replace {{file}} with this version? Current file will be snapshotted first.
folders.docxWarning: If the file is open in Word, close it before restoring.

activity.title: Recent changes
activity.subtitle: Every snapshot AutoVersion has taken, newest first.
activity.empty: No changes yet. Edit a file in any of your watched folders to see it here.

howItWorks.title: How AutoVersion works
howItWorks.intro: AutoVersion sits in your menu bar and watches the folders you tell it to protect. Every time a file is saved, it takes a snapshot — a complete copy of that file at that moment.
howItWorks.snapshot: A snapshot is a full copy of one file at one point in time, stored inside AutoVersion (not in your folder). It is independent of every other snapshot, so deleting one won't break the others.
howItWorks.debounceTitle: Why doesn't every save create a snapshot?
howItWorks.debounce: When you hit save in apps like Microsoft Word, the app actually performs several rapid file operations under the hood. AutoVersion waits about 2 seconds for those to settle before snapshotting, so one save by you produces one snapshot.
howItWorks.dedupTitle: What if I save without changing anything?
howItWorks.dedup: AutoVersion fingerprints each file using SHA-256. If the new content is identical to the previous snapshot, no new snapshot is created.

restoring.title: Restoring a version
restoring.intro: Bring any file back to a previous state with two clicks.
restoring.step1: Open the Folders tab, pick a folder, then a file.
restoring.step2: Click any snapshot in the history list to preview the diff.
restoring.safety: Before AutoVersion overwrites your file, it takes a snapshot of the current state. So restoring is never destructive — you can always restore back to where you were.
restoring.word: If a .docx file is open in Microsoft Word, close it first. Word holds an exclusive lock on open documents and will refuse the file replacement.

pauseResume.title: Pause and resume
pauseResume.intro: Sometimes you don't want AutoVersion taking snapshots — for example while running a script that touches many files at once.
pauseResume.where: You can pause from two places:
pauseResume.tray: The menu bar icon → Pause Watching.
pauseResume.behavior: While paused, AutoVersion ignores file save events. Existing snapshots stay intact and remain restorable. Resume to start protecting again.

dataLocation.title: Where AutoVersion stores your data
dataLocation.intro: Everything AutoVersion creates lives under your home directory. Nothing is uploaded anywhere.
dataLocation.config: Stored at ~/Library/Application Support/AutoVersion/config.json — a single small JSON file with your watched folders and preferences.
dataLocation.snapshots: Each watched folder gets its own snapshot store under ~/Library/Application Support/AutoVersion/repos/<folder-id>/.
dataLocation.logs: Diagnostic logs go to ~/Library/Logs/AutoVersion/autoversion.log. Useful if you ever need to file a bug report.
dataLocation.nuke: If you ever want to start fresh, quit AutoVersion and run: rm -rf ~/Library/Application\ Support/AutoVersion. Your original files in the watched folders are untouched.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.noSnapshots: Check, in this order: that the folder's Enabled toggle is on; that the file's extension is in the watched list; that AutoVersion is not paused (look at the menu bar icon); and the log file at ~/Library/Logs/AutoVersion/autoversion.log for clues.
troubleshooting.manyVersionsTitle: I got 10 versions for one Word save
troubleshooting.manyVersions: That shouldn't happen — AutoVersion's debouncer collapses Word's save sequence into one snapshot. If you see this, please report it as a bug and include a few lines from the log file.
troubleshooting.gatekeeperTitle: First launch is blocked by Gatekeeper
troubleshooting.gatekeeper: AutoVersion is unsigned. Right-click the app and choose Open the first time, or run xattr -cr /Applications/AutoVersion.app from Terminal.

about.title: About
about.description: AutoVersion keeps automatic snapshots of files in folders you choose. Snapshots live under ~/Library/Application Support/AutoVersion/repos/.
about.gatekeeper: Unsigned macOS build: first open with right-click → Open, or xattr -cr /Applications/AutoVersion.app.
